_comment: Auto-generated from veda-attribute-master-export.xlsx. Do not edit.

ACT_BND.description: Bound on the overall activity a process.
ACT_BND.units_ranges_defaults: Units of activity [0,?); default value: none Default i/e[6]: MIG
ACT_BND.instances_conditions: Since inter-/extrapolation default is MIG, the bound must be explicitly specified for each period, unless an inter-/extrapolation option is set. If the bound is specified for a timeslice s above the process timeslice resolution (prc_tsl), the bound is applied to the sum of the activity variables according to the timeslice tree. Standard aggregation.
ACT_BND.affected_equations_or_variables: Activity limit constraint (EQ(l)_ACTBND) when s is above prc_tsl. Direct bound on activity variable (VAR_ACT) when at the prc_tsl level.

ACT_COST.description: Variable costs associated with the activity of a process.
ACT_COST.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per unit of activity [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
ACT_COST.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the activity variable (VAR_ACT) as a component of the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR). May appear in user constraints (EQ_UC*) if specified in UC_NAME.

ACT_CSTPL.description: Partial load cost penalty, defined as an additional cost per activity at the minimum operating level, corresponding to the efficiency loss at that load level. Added as an extra term to variable costs in the objective and reporting.
ACT_CSTPL.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per unit of activity [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
ACT_CSTPL.instances_conditions: Used as an alternative or supplement to using ACT_LOSPL(r,y,p,'FX'). When used as an alternative, the fuel increase at the minimum operating level that should be included in the cost penalty must be embedded in the ACT_CSTPL coefficient.
ACT_CSTPL.affected_equations_or_variables: Generates an additional term in EQ_OBJVAR for the increase in operating cost.

ACT_CSTRMP.description: Defines ramp-up (L=UP) or ramp-down (L=LO) cost per unit of load change (in capacity units). For load-shifting processes defines the cost of shifting one unit of load by one hour, forward (UP) or backward (LO).
ACT_CSTRMP.units_ranges_defaults: Corrency unit per unit of capacity (change in load) [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
ACT_CSTRMP.instances_conditions: Can be used for standard processes in basic, advanced and discrete unit commitment extensions. Can also be used for load-shifting processes for defining the cost of shifting loads.
ACT_CSTRMP.affected_equations_or_variables: Activates generation of EQ_ACTRMPC. Generates an additional term in EQ_OBJVAR for the increase in operating cost.

ACT_CSTSD.description: Defines start-up (bd=UP) and shutdown costs (bd=LO) per unit of started-up capacity, differentiated by start-up type (upt). The start-up type of a power plant depends on its non-operational time after shut-down, as defined by using ACT_MAXNON.
ACT_CSTSD.units_ranges_defaults: Currency units per unit of started-up capacity  [0,?); Default value: none Default i/e: STD
ACT_CSTSD.instances_conditions: Activates the advanced unit commitment option. In the case of the shut-down costs, only the tuple (upt, bd) = (HOT, LO) is a valid instance for this parameter. Requires the parameter ACT_MAXNON to be defined as well.
ACT_CSTSD.affected_equations_or_variables: Generates an additional term in EQ_OBJVAR for the increase in operating cost.

ACT_CSTUP.description: Cost of process start-up per unit of started-up capacity. Added as an extra term to variable costs in the objective and reporting.
ACT_CSTUP.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per unit of capacity [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
ACT_CSTUP.instances_conditions: The tslvl level refers to the timeslice cycle for which the start-up cost is defined. Only applicable when the min. stable operating level has been defined with ACT_MINLD.
ACT_CSTUP.affected_equations_or_variables: Activates generation of EQL_ACTUPS eqs. Generates an additional term in the variable operating costs included in EQ_OBJVAR.

ACT_CUM.description: Bound on the cumulative amount of annual process activity between the years y1 and y2, within a region.
ACT_CUM.units_ranges_defaults: Activity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: N/A
ACT_CUM.instances_conditions: The years y1 and y2 may be any years of the set allyear; where y1 may also be 'BOH' for first year of first period and y2 may be 'EOH' for last year of last period.
ACT_CUM.affected_equations_or_variables: Generates an instance of the cumulative constraint (EQ_CUMFLO)

ACT_EFF.description: Activity efficiency for process, i.e. amount of activity per unit of commodity flows in the group cg. For more information on usage, see Section 6.3 for details about EQE_ACTEFF.
ACT_EFF.units_ranges_defaults: Activity unit per flow unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
ACT_EFF.instances_conditions: The group cg may be a single commodity, group, or commodity type on the shadow side, or a single commodity in the PCG; cg='ACT' refers to the default shadow group. If no group efficiency is defined, shadow group is assumed to be the commodity type. Individual commodity efficiencies are multiplied with the shadow group efficiency (default=1). Direct inheritance. Weighted aggregation.
ACT_EFF.affected_equations_or_variables: Generates instances of the activity efficiency constraint (EQE_ACTEFF)

ACT_FLO.description: Flow of commodities in cg in proportion to activity, in timeslice s.
ACT_FLO.units_ranges_defaults: Flow unit per activity unit  [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
ACT_FLO.instances_conditions: Inherited/aggregated to the timeslice levels of the process activity. Direct inheritance. Weighted aggregation.
ACT_FLO.affected_equations_or_variables: Establishes a transfor­mation relationship (EQ_PTRANS) between the flows in the PCG and one or more input (or output) commodities.

ACT_LOSPL.description: Partial load efficiency parameters. 1) (bd='FX'): Proportional increase in specific fuel con­sumption at minimum operating level 2) (bd='LO'): Minimum operating level of partial load operation 3) (bd='UP'): Fraction of feasible load range above the minimum operating level, below which the efficiency losses are assumed to occur.
ACT_LOSPL.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,?); default values:  FX: none LO: default value is ACT_MINLD or 0.1 if that is not defined UP: 0.6 Default i/e: STD
ACT_LOSPL.instances_conditions: Endogenous partial load modeling can only be used for processes that have their efficiency modelled by the ACT_EFF parameter, which must be defined on the shadow side of the process. For other processes, the ACT_CSTPL parameter can be used for modeling a cost penalty at partial loads.
ACT_LOSPL.affected_equations_or_variables: Generates instances of the partial load efficiency constraint EQ_ACTPL.

ACT_LOSSD.description: Used for modeling endogenous partial load efficiency losses during the start-up and shut-down phase. ·   With bd=UP defines increase in specific fuel con­sump­tion at the start up load level defined by the ratio ACT_MINLD / ACT_SDTIME(upt,'UP') for start-up type upt; ·   With bd=LO defines the increase in specific fuel consump­tion at the start up load level defined by the ratio ACT_MINLD / ACT_SDTIME('HOT', 'LO').
ACT_LOSSD.units_ranges_defaults: Dimensionless [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
ACT_LOSSD.instances_conditions: Can only be used when the advanced unit commitment option is used for the process (therefore, defining both ACT_CSTSD and ACT_MAXNON is required) Requires also that ACT_EFF has been used for defining the process efficiency (on the shadow side of the process).

ACT_MAXNON.description: Max. non-operational time before transition to next stand-by condition, by start-up type, in hours ·   Defines the max. non-operational time before a subsequent start-up of type upt.
ACT_MAXNON.units_ranges_defaults: hours [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
ACT_MAXNON.instances_conditions: Can only be used when the advanced unit commitment option is used for the process (thus defining ACT_CSTSD is required)

ACT_MINLD.description: Minimum stable operating level of a dispatchable process.
ACT_MINLD.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
ACT_MINLD.instances_conditions: Can only be used for standard processes (not IRE or STG). Must be defined if ACT_CSTUP or ACT_TIME is specified.
ACT_MINLD.affected_equations_or_variables: Generates instances of equations EQ_CAPLOAD and EQE_ACTUPS.

ACT_SDTIME.description: Defines the duration of start-up (bd=UP) and shut-down (bd=LO) phases, by start-up type, in hours.
ACT_SDTIME.units_ranges_defaults: hours [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
ACT_SDTIME.instances_conditions: Can only be used when ACT_CSTSD is specified for the process (advanced unit commitment option) When specifying the duration of the shut-down phase, only the tuple (upt,bd)=(HOT,LO) is valid
ACT_SDTIME.affected_equations_or_variables: Activates generation of EQ_SUDTIME, and  used also in the equations EQ_ACTPL EQ_SDSLANT EQ_SDMINON EQ_SUDPLL

ACT_TIME.description: 1) Minimum online  (UP) / offline (LO)  hours of a process  with start-up costs  modeled (lim=LO/UP)  2) Maximum number  of start-up cycles  within process time­slice cycles (lim=N). 3) Maximum delay or advance of load shift (lim=UP/LO/FX) or load balancing time (lim=N) for a load-shifting storage.
ACT_TIME.units_ranges_defaults: Hours  [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
ACT_TIME.instances_conditions: Can be used for standard processes when start-up costs have been modeled, using both ACT_MINLD and  ACT_CSTUP at the  DAYNITE/WEEKLY level. The lim type 'FX' is not supported for this use, and is ignored. Can also be used for load-shifting storage processes, for defining the maximum delay/advance of load shift, or the time-window for load balancing (cf. Sect. 4.3.9).
ACT_TIME.affected_equations_or_variables: Generates instances of EQL_ACTUPC. For load-shifting storage processes, generates instances of EQ_SLSIFT.

ACT_UPS.description: Maximum ramp-rate (down/up) of process activity as a fraction of nominal on-line capacity per hour.
ACT_UPS.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
ACT_UPS.instances_conditions: Inherited/aggregated to the timeslice levels of the process activity. Direct inheritance. Weighted aggregation. The ramp rates can only be specified with bd=LO/UP.

BS_CAPACT.description: Conversion factor from exogenous reserve demand to activity

BS_DELTA.description: Calibration parameters for probabilistic reserve demands

BS_DEMDET.description: Deterministic demands of reserves - EXOGEN and WMAXSI

BS_LAMBDA.description: Fudge factors for dependencies in reserve requirements

BS_OMEGA.description: Indicator of how to define reserve demand from deterministic and probabilistic component

BS_RMAX.description: Maximum contribution of process p to provision of reserve c as a fraction of capacity

BS_RTYPE.description: Types of reserve commodities, positive or negative 1-4

BS_SIGMA.description: Standard deviation of imbalance source ITEM

BS_STIME.description: Minimum times for reserve provision form storage (hours

CAP_BND.description: Bound on investment in new capacity.
CAP_BND.units_ranges_defaults: Capacity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: MIG
CAP_BND.instances_conditions: Since inter-/extrapolation is default is MIG, a bound must be specified for each period desired, if no explicit inter-/extrapolation option is given.
CAP_BND.affected_equations_or_variables: Imposes an indirect limit on the capacity transfer equation (EQ_CPT) by means of a direct bound on the capacity variable (VAR_CAP).

CCAP0.description: The initial cumulative capacity (starting point on thelearning curve) for a (non-resource) technology that ismodeled as one for which endogenous technologylearning (ETL) applies

CCAPM.description: The maximum cumulative capacity (ending point onthe learning curve) for a (non-resource) technology that is modeled as one for which endogenous technologylearning (ETL) applies

CLUSTER.description: The “cluster mapping and coupling factor” for atechnology that is modeled as a clustered technology isassociated with a key learning technology to whichendogenous technology learning (ETL) applies

CM_CONST.description: Various climate module constants, e.g. phi and sigma values between reservoirs.
CM_CONST.units_ranges_defaults: Constant specific unit [open]; default value: See Appendix Default i/e: N/A
CM_CONST.instances_conditions: See Appendix on Climate Module for details.

CM_EXOFORC.units_ranges_defaults: Forcing unit [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
CM_EXOFORC.instances_conditions: Default values are provided. See Appendix on Climate Module for details.

CM_GHGMAP.description: Mapping and conversion of regional GHG emissions to global emissions in the climate module
CM_GHGMAP.units_ranges_defaults: Units of climate module emissions per units of regional emissions  [0, ?); default value: none
CM_GHGMAP.instances_conditions: The global emissions in the climate module (cm_var) are 'CO2-GtC' (GtC), 'CH4-Mt' (Mt) and 'N2O-Mt' (Mt). See Appendix on Climate Module for details.

CM_HISTORY.description: Calibration values for CO2 and forcing
CM_HISTORY.units_ranges_defaults: Climate variable unit [0, ?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
CM_HISTORY.instances_conditions: Default values are provided until 2010. See Appendix on Climate Module for details.

CM_LINFOR.units_ranges_defaults: Forcing unit per concentration unit [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
CM_LINFOR.instances_conditions: With lim types LO/UP, CO2 forcing function can be automatically linearized between the concent­ration levels given. For CH4 and N2O, lim types FX/N must be used (N=con­cent­ration multiplier, FX=constant term). See Appendix on Climate Module for details.

CM_MAXC.units_ranges_defaults: Climate variable unit [0, ?); default value: none Default i/e: none
CM_MAXC.instances_conditions: Since no default inter-/extrapolation, bounds must be explicitly specified for each desired year, unless an explicit inter-/extrapolation option is set. See Appendix on Climate Module for details.

COM_AGG.description: Aggregation of commodity NET/PRD production to the production side of the balance of another commodity.
COM_AGG.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity units [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
COM_AGG.instances_conditions: When commodity type is LO, VAR_COMNET of c1 is aggregated to c2;  When commodity type is FX/N, VAR_COMPRD of c1 is aggregated to c2.
COM_AGG.affected_equations_or_variables: Adds a term in EQ(l)_COMBAL and EQ(l)_COMPRD.

COM_BNDNET.description: Limit on the net amount of a commodity within a region for a particular timeslice.
COM_BNDNET.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit [open]; default value: none Default i/e: MIG
COM_BNDNET.instances_conditions: Since inter-/extrapolation default is MIG, a bound must be specified for each period desired, if no explicit inter-/extrapolation option is given. If the bound is specified for a timeslice s above the commodity timeslice resolution (com_tsl), the bound is applied to the sum of the net commodity variables (VAR_COMNET) below it, according to the timeslice tree. Standard aggregation.
COM_BNDNET.affected_equations_or_variables: The balance constraint is set to an equality (EQE_COMBAL). Either the finer timeslice variables are summed (EQ(l)_BNDNET) or the bound applied direct to the commodity net variable(VAR_COMNET) when at the commodity level (com_tsl).

COM_BNDPRD.description: Limit on the amount of a commodity produced within a region for a particular timeslice.
COM_BNDPRD.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: MIG
COM_BNDPRD.instances_conditions: Since inter-/extrapolation default is MIG, a bound must be specified for each period desired, if no explicit inter-/extrapolation option is given. If the bound is specified for a timeslice s being above the commodity timeslice resolution (com_tsl), the bound is applied to the sum of the commodity production variables (VAR_COMPRD) below it, according to the timeslice tree. Standard aggregation.
COM_BNDPRD.affected_equations_or_variables: The balance constraint is set to an equality (EQE_COMBAL). Finer timeslice variables summed (EQ(l)_BNDPRD). or the bound is applied direct to the commodity production variable (VAR_COMPRD) when at the commodity level (com_tsl).

COM_BPRICE.description: Base price of a demand commodity for the elastic demand formulation.
COM_BPRICE.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per commodity unit [open]; default value: none Default i/e: none
COM_BPRICE.instances_conditions: The control parameter $SET TIMESED ‘YES’ to activate elastic demands must be set.
COM_BPRICE.affected_equations_or_variables: Controls the inclusion of the elastic demand variable (VAR_ELAST) in the commodity balance equation(EQ(l)_COMBAL) Applied to the elastic demand variable (VAR_ELAST) in the objective function (EQ_OBJELS).

COM_CSTBAL.description: for defining a cost for the generation (type=PRD), imports (IMP), exports (EXP), net imports (NTX), consumption (CON), or net positive generation (NPG) at node N, optionally differentiated by timeslice s (unit: currency unit / energy unit).

COM_CSTNET.description: Cost on the net amount of a commodity within a region for a particular timeslice.
COM_CSTNET.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per commodity unit [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
COM_CSTNET.affected_equations_or_variables: Forces the net commodity variable (VAR_COMNET) to be included in the equality balance constraint (EQE_COMBAL). Applied to said variable in the cost component of the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR).

COM_CSTPRD.description: Cost on the production of a commodity, within a region for a particular timeslice.
COM_CSTPRD.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per commodity unit [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
COM_CSTPRD.affected_equations_or_variables: Forces the commodity production variable (VAR_COMPRD) to be included in the equality balance constraint (EQE_COMBAL). Applied to said variable in the cost component of the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR).

COM_CUMNET.description: Bound on the cumulative net amount of a commodity between the years y1 and y2, within a region for a particular timeslice.
COM_CUMNET.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: not possible
COM_CUMNET.instances_conditions: The years y1 and y2 may be any years of the set allyear; where y1 may also be ‘BOH’ for first year of first period and y2 may be ‘EOH’ for last year of last period.
COM_CUMNET.affected_equations_or_variables: Forces the net commodity variable (VAR_COMNET) to be included in the equality balance constraint (EQE_COMBAL). Generates the cumulative commodity constraint (EQ(l)_CUMNET).

COM_CUMPRD.description: Bound on the cumulative production of a commodity between the years y1 and y2 within a region for a particular timeslice.
COM_CUMPRD.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: not possible
COM_CUMPRD.instances_conditions: The years y1 and y2 may be any years of the set allyear; where y1 may also be ‘BOH’ for first year of first period and y2 may be ‘EOH’ for last year of last period.
COM_CUMPRD.affected_equations_or_variables: Forces the net commodity variable (VAR_COMPRD) to be included in the balance equation (EQE_COMBAL). The cumulative constraint is generated (EQ(l)_CUMPRD).

COM_ELAST.description: Elasticity of demand indicating how much the demand rises/falls in response to a unit change in the marginal cost of meeting a demand that is elastic. See also Appendix D for additional details on defining demand functions.
COM_ELAST.units_ranges_defaults: Dimensionless [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
COM_ELAST.instances_conditions: The control parameter  $SET TIMESED YES must be set to activate elastic demands. An elasticity is required for each direction the demand is permitted to move.  The index lim = 'LO' corresponds to demand decrease, while lim = 'UP' denotes the direction for demand increase.  A different value may be provided for each direction, thus curves may be asymmetric. Substitution elasticities can be defined with lim='N', among a group of demands aggregated by COM_AGG.
COM_ELAST.affected_equations_or_variables: Controls the inclusion of the elastic demand variable (VAR_ELAST) in the commodity balance equation(EQ(l)_COMBAL)  Applied to the elastic demand variable (VAR_ELAST) in the objective function costs (EQ_OBJELS).

COM_ELASTX.description: Shape index for the elasticity of demand
COM_ELASTX.units_ranges_defaults: Integer scalar  [1,999]; default value: none Default extrapolation: MIG
COM_ELASTX.instances_conditions: Provided when shaping of elasticity based upon demand level is desired. Note: Shape index 1 is reserved for constant 1.
COM_ELASTX.affected_equations_or_variables: Affects the demand elasticities applied in EQ_OBJELS

COM_FR.description: Fraction of the annual demand (COM_PROJ) or commodity flow occurring in timeslice s; describes the shape of the load curve.
COM_FR.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,1]; default value: timeslice duration (G_YRFR) Default i/e: STD
COM_FR.instances_conditions: Normally defined only for demand commodities (com_type = 'DEM'), but can be applied to any commodity for defining load profiles. Affects timeslice resolution at which a commodity is tracked (RTCS_TSFR), and thereby may affect when a process cannot operate (rtps_off). Weighted inheritance. Weighted aggregation.
COM_FR.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the annual demand (COM_PROJ) as the RHS of the balance equation (EQ(l)_COMBAL). Enters the peaking equation (EQ_PEAK), if a peaking commodity. Applied to the bounds of elastic demand step variables (VAR_ELAST). Applied via RTFCS_FR in all equations to flows having a timeslice level coarser than target level.

COM_IE.description: Infrastructure or transmission efficiency of a commodity.
COM_IE.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction (0,?); default value: 1 Default i/e: STD
COM_IE.affected_equations_or_variables: Overall efficiency applied to the total production of a commodity in the commodity balance equation (EQ(l)_COMBAL).

COM_MSHGV.description: Defines commodity com as a market where the market sharing mechanism is to be applied. The parameter value defines the heterogeneity parameter.

COM_PEAK.description: Set of pairs {r,cg} such that a peaking constraint is to be generated for commodity cg in region r. [this is a switch; >0 => YES]

COM_PKFLX.description: Difference between the average demand and the peak demand in timeslice s, expressed as fraction of the average demand.
COM_PKFLX.units_ranges_defaults: Scalar [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
COM_PKFLX.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the total consumption of a commodity to raise the capacity needed to satisfy the peaking constraint (EQ_PEAK).

COM_PKRSV.description: Peak reserve margin as fraction of peak demand, e.g. if COM_PKRSV = 0.2, the total installed capacity must exceed the peak load by 20%.
COM_PKRSV.units_ranges_defaults: Scalar [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
COM_PKRSV.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the total consumption of a commodity to raise the capacity needed to satisfy the peaking constraint (EQ_PEAK).

COM_PROJ.description: Projected annual demand for a commodity.
COM_PROJ.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
COM_PROJ.affected_equations_or_variables: Serves as the RHS (after COM_FR applied) of the commodity balance constraint (EQ(l)_COMBAL). Enters the peaking equation (EQ_PEAK), if a peaking commodity. Applied when setting the upper bound of an elastic demand step (VAR_ELAST).

COM_STEP.description: Number of steps to use for the approximation of change of producer/consumer surplus when using the linearized elastic demand formulations.
COM_STEP.instances_conditions: The control parameter $SET TIMESED ‘YES’ must be set to activate elastic demands. The number of steps is required for each direction the demand is permitted to move. The index bd=LO denotes the direction of demand decrease, bd=UP increase, and bd=FX is a shortcut for both. A different value may be provided for each direction, thus curves may be asymmetric.
COM_STEP.affected_equations_or_variables: Controls the instance of the elastic demand variable (VAR_ELAST) in: the commodity balance equation (EQ(l)_COMBAL); setting of the step limit for the elastic demand variable (VAR_ELAST); enters the objective function costs (EQ_OBJELS).

COM_SUBNET.description: Subsidy on the net amount of a commodity within a region for a particular timeslice.
COM_SUBNET.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
COM_SUBNET.affected_equations_or_variables: Forces the net commodity variable (VAR_COMNET) to be included in the equality balance constraint (EQE_COMBAL). Applied (-) to said variable in the cost component of the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR).

COM_SUBPRD.description: Subsidy on the production of a commodity within a region for a particular timeslice.
COM_SUBPRD.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
COM_SUBPRD.affected_equations_or_variables: Forces the commodity production variable (VAR_COMPRD) to be included in the equality balance constraint (EQE_COMBAL). Applied (-) to said variable in the cost component of the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR).

COM_TAXNET.description: Tax on the net amount of a commodity within a region for a particular timeslice.
COM_TAXNET.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
COM_TAXNET.affected_equations_or_variables: Forces the net commodity variable (VAR_COMNET) to be included in the equality balance constraint (EQE_COMBAL). Applied to said variable in the cost component of the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR).

COM_TAXPRD.description: Tax on the production of a commodity within a region for a particular timeslice.
COM_TAXPRD.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
COM_TAXPRD.affected_equations_or_variables: Forces the commodity production variable (VAR_COMPRD) to be included in the equality balance constraint (EQE_COMBAL). Applied to said variable in the cost component of the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR).

COM_VOC.description: Possible variation of demand in both directions when using the elastic demand formulation.
COM_VOC.units_ranges_defaults: Dimensionless [0,?); default: none Default i/e: STD
COM_VOC.instances_conditions: The control parameter $SET TIMESED ‘YES’ to activate elastic demands must be set. A number is required for each direction the demand is permitted to move. The index bd = LO corresponds to the direction of decreasing the demand, while bd = UP denotes the direction for demand increase. A different value may be provided for each direction, thus curves may be asymmetric.
COM_VOC.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied when setting the bound of an elastic demand step (VAR_ELAST). Applied to the elasticity variable in the objective function costs (EQ_OBJELS).

CmdF_GAMS.description: To append parameters to GAMS Call in VTRun.CMD file (multiple declarations will be concatenated)

CmdF_Title.description: To append text to command window title; 0 => no title

CmdF_bot.description: To append statements after GDX2VEDA call in VTRun.CMD

CmdF_top.description: To append statements before the GAMS call in VTRun.CMD

Com_OFF.description: Specifying that the commodity c in region r is not available between the years y1 and y2 [set of quadruplets {r,c,y1,y2} such that commodity c is unavailable from years y1 to y1 in region r] [this is a switch; >0 => YES]

Com_PKTS.description: Set of triplets {r,cg,s} such that a peaking constraint for a single commodity or a group of commodities cg [this is a switch; >0 => YES]

DAM_BQTY.description: Base quantity of emissions for damage cost accounting
DAM_BQTY.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: N/A
DAM_BQTY.instances_conditions: Only effective when DAM_COST has been defined for commodity c.

DAM_COST.description: Marginal damage cost of emissions at Base quantity.
DAM_COST.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
DAM_COST.instances_conditions: Damage costs are by default endogenous (included in the objective). To set them exogenous, use $SET DAMAGE NO

DAM_ELAST.description: Elasticity of damage cost in the lower or upper direction from Base quantity.
DAM_ELAST.units_ranges_defaults: Dimensionless [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: N/A
DAM_ELAST.instances_conditions: Only effective when DAM_COST has been defined for commodity c.

DAM_STEP.description: Number of steps for linearizing damage costs in the lower or upper direction from Base quantity.
DAM_STEP.units_ranges_defaults: Integer number [1,?); default value: none Default i/e: N/A
DAM_STEP.instances_conditions: Only effective when DAM_COST has been defined for commodity c.

DAM_VOC.description: Variance of emissions in the lower or upper direction from Base quantity as a fraction of Base quantity.
DAM_VOC.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction LO: [0,1]; UP: [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: N/A
DAM_VOC.instances_conditions: Only effective when DAM_COST has been defined for commodity c.

DECAYr.description: NOT TO BE USED IN TIMES. THIS IS ONLY TO MIGRATE MARKAL MODELS.

END.description: The last year when a technology is available for Investment

FLO_BND.description: Bound on the flow of a commodity or the sum of flows within a commodity group.
FLO_BND.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit [0,?); default: none Default i/e: MIG
FLO_BND.instances_conditions: If the bound is specified for a timeslice s being above the flow timeslice resolution (rtpcs_varf), the bound is applied to the sum of the flow variables (VAR_FLO) according to the timeslice tree, otherwise directly to the flow variable. No aggregation.[7]
FLO_BND.affected_equations_or_variables: Flow activity limit constraint (EQ(l)_FLOBND) when s is above rtpcs_varf Direct bound on activity variable (VAR_FLO) when at the rtpcs_varf level.

FLO_COST.description: Variable cost of a process associated with the production/ consumption of a commodity.
FLO_COST.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per commodity unit [open]; default: none Default i/e: STD
FLO_COST.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the flow variable (VAR_FLO) when entering the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR). May appear in user constraints (EQ_UC*) if specified in UC_NAME.

FLO_CUM.description: Bound on the cumulative amount of annual process activity between the years y1 and y2, within a region.
FLO_CUM.units_ranges_defaults: Flow unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: N/A
FLO_CUM.instances_conditions: The years y1 and y2 may be any years of the set allyear; where y1 may also be 'BOH' for first year of first period and y2 may be 'EOH' for last year of last period.
FLO_CUM.affected_equations_or_variables: Generates an instance of the cumulative constraint (EQ_CUMFLO)

FLO_DELIV.description: Cost of a delivering (consuming) a commodity to a process.
FLO_DELIV.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per commodity unit [open]; default: none Default i/e: STD
FLO_DELIV.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the flow variable (VAR_FLO) when entering the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR). May appear in user constraints (EQ_UC*) if specified in UC_NAME.

FLO_EMIS.description: Defines the amount of emissions (c) per unit of process flow(s) or activity (cg).
FLO_EMIS.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit of c / commodity unit of cg [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
FLO_EMIS.instances_conditions: See FLO_EFF. If com is of type ENV and is not in the process topology, it is added to it as an output flow.

FLO_FR.description: 1) Bounds the flow of commodity (c) entering or leaving process (p) in a timeslice, in pro­por­tion to annual flow. 2) If specified also at the ANNUAL level, bounds the flow level in proportion to the average level under the parent timeslice
FLO_FR.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,1] / [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: MIG
FLO_FR.instances_conditions: FLO_FR may be specified as lower, upper or fixed bounds, in contrast to COM_FR. Can be specified for any flow variable having a subannual timeslice resolution. Weighted aggregation. Direct inheritance, if defined at the ANNUAL level.
FLO_FR.affected_equations_or_variables: A share equation (EQ(l)_FLOFR) limiting the amount of commodity (c) is generated according to the bound type (bd = l indicator).

FLO_FUNC.description: A key parameter describing the basic operation of or within a process. Sets the ratio between the sum of flows in commodity group cg2 to the sum of flows in commodity group cg1, thereby defining the efficiency of producing cg2 from cg1 (subject to any FLO_SUM). cg1 and cg2 may be also single commodities.
FLO_FUNC.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit of cg2/commodity unit of cg1 [open]; default value: see next column Default i/e: STD
FLO_FUNC.instances_conditions: If for the same indexes the parameter FLO_SUM is specified but no FLO_FUNC, the FLO_FUNC is set to 1. Important factor in determining the level at which a process operates in that the derived transformation parameter (COEF_PTRAN) is inherited/aggregated to the timeslice levels of the flow variables associated with the commodities in the group cg1.
FLO_FUNC.affected_equations_or_variables: Establishes the basic transformation relationship (EQ_PTRANS) between one or more input (or output) commodities and one or more output (or input) commodities.  Establishes the relationship between storage charging / discharging and a related commodity flow (VAR_FLO) in the auxiliary storage flow equation (EQ_STGAUX).

FLO_FUNCX.description: Age-based shaping curve (SHAPE) to be applied to the flow parameters   (ACT_EFF/ACT_FLO/ FLO_FUNC/FLO_SUM/FLO_EMIS/FLO_EFF)
FLO_FUNCX.units_ranges_defaults: Integer scalar  [1,999]; default value: none Default extrapolation: MIG
FLO_FUNCX.instances_conditions: Provided when shaping based upon age is desired. Vintaged processes only. Note: Shape index 1 is reserved for constant 1. ACT_EFF(cg): cg1=cg, cg2='ACT' ACT_FLO(cg): cg1='ACT', cg2=cg FLO_EMIS(cg,c): cg1=cg2=c FLO_EFF(cg,c): cg1=cg2=c FLO_FUNC(cg1,cg2): cgN=cgN
FLO_FUNCX.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the flow variable (VAR_FLO) in a transformation equation (EQ_PTRANS / EQE_ACTEFF) to account for changes in the transfor­mation efficiency according to the age of each process vintage.

FLO_MARK.description: Process-wise market share in total commodity production.
FLO_MARK.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,1]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
FLO_MARK.instances_conditions: The same given fraction is applied to all time­slices of the commodity (this could be generalized to allow time-slice-specific fractions, if deemed useful). If an ANNUAL level market-share is desired for a timesliced commodity, PRC_MARK can be used instead.
FLO_MARK.affected_equations_or_variables: The individual process flow variables (VAR_FLO, VAR_IN, VAR_STGIN/OUT) are constrained (EQ(l)_FLOMRK) to a fraction of the total production of a commodity (VAR_COMPRD). Forces the commodity production variable (VAR_COMPRD) to be included in the equality balance constraint (EQE_COMBAL).

FLO_PKCOI.description: Factor that permits attributing more (or less) demand to the peaking equation (EQ_PEAK) than the average demand calculated by the model, to handle the situation where peak usage is typically higher (or lower) due to coincidental (or non-coincidental) loads at the time of the peak demand.
FLO_PKCOI.units_ranges_defaults: Scalar [open]; default value: 1 Default i/e: STD
FLO_PKCOI.instances_conditions: FLO_PKCOI is specified for individual processes p consuming the peak commodity c. Direct inheritance. Weighted aggregation.  Used when the timeslices are not necessarily fine enough to pick up the actual peak within the peak timeslices.
FLO_PKCOI.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the flow variable (VAR_FLO) to adjust the amount of a commodity consumed when considering the average demand contributing to the peaking constraint (EQ_PEAK).

FLO_SHAR.description: Share of flow commodity c based upon the sum of individual flows defined by the commodity group cg belonging to process p.
FLO_SHAR.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,1]; default value: none Default i/e: MIG over milestoneyears, STD over pastyears
FLO_SHAR.instances_conditions: Direct inheritance. Weighted aggregation. A common example of using FLO_SHAR is to specify the power-to-heat ratio of CHP plants in the backpressure point. For example, for a heat output of a CHP technology, the FLO_SHAR parameter would have the value CHPR/(1+CHPR), with CHPR being the heat-to-power ratio.
FLO_SHAR.affected_equations_or_variables: When the commodity is an input an EQ(l)_INSHR equation is generated. When the commodity is an output an EQ(l)_OUTSHR equation is generated.

FLO_SUB.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
FLO_SUB.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied with a minus sign to the flow variable (VAR_FLO) when entering the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR). May appear in user constraints (EQ_UC*) if specified in UC_NAME.

FLO_TAX.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per commodity unit [0,?); default: none Default i/e: STD
FLO_TAX.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the flow variable (VAR_FLO) when entering the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR). May appear in user constraints (EQ_UC*) if specified in UC_NAME.

GROWTH.description: NOT TO BE USED IN TIMES. THIS IS ONLY TO MIGRATE MARKAL MODELS.

GROWTH_TID.description: NOT TO BE USED IN TIMES. THIS IS ONLY TO MIGRATE MARKAL MODELS.

GROWTH_TIDr.description: NOT TO BE USED IN TIMES. THIS IS ONLY TO MIGRATE MARKAL MODELS.

GROWTHr.description: NOT TO BE USED IN TIMES. THIS IS ONLY TO MIGRATE MARKAL MODELS.

GR_DEMFR.description: Fraction of the total demand (by timeslice) to be allocated to node (Commodity)

GR_ENDFR.description: Fraction of a sectoral demand (CommGrp) to be allocated to node (Commodity)

GR_GENFR.description: Fraction of the unit type (CommGrp) generation to be allocated to node (Commodity)

GR_GENLEV.description: Generation level indicator for a generated electricity commodity

GR_GENMAP.description: Mapping of generating technology to unit type or load category (CommGrp)

GR_THMIN.description: Minimum level of thermal generation as a fraction of the nominal capacity of process p in year y (3 options - see doc)

GR_VARGEN.description: Expected variation of load category lc in timeslice s and direction bd (bd = lo/up)

GR_XBND.description: Maximum power level of net imports to / exports from a region

G_CUREX.description: Conversion factor from currency cur1 to currency cur2, with cur2 to be used in the objective function.
G_CUREX.instances_conditions: The target currency cur2 must have a discount rate defined with G_DRATE.

G_CYCLE.description: Defines the total number of cycles on level tslvl, in a year. Provides default values for TS_CYCLE (see entry for that).
G_CYCLE.units_ranges_defaults: Number of cycles [1,?); Default values:  ·  1 for ANNUAL ·  1 for SEASON ·  52 for WEEKLY  ·  365 for DAYNITE
G_CYCLE.instances_conditions: Not recommended to be changed; use TS_CYCLE instead, whenever the timeslice cycles are different from the default, because changing G_CYCLE would change the meaning of storage availability factors.
G_CYCLE.affected_equations_or_variables: Affects inter­pretation of availability factors for the storage level, whenever capacity represents the maximum nominal output level (EQ(l)_CAPACT, EQL_CAPFLO).

G_DRATE.description: System-wide discount rate in region r for each time-period.
G_DRATE.related_sets_and_parameters: OBJ_DISC, OBJ_DCEOH, NCAP_DRATE, COR_SALVI, COR_SALVD, COEF_PVT, VDA_DISC
G_DRATE.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction (0,1]; default value = none Default i/e: STD
G_DRATE.instances_conditions: A value must be provided for each region. Interpolation is dense (all individual years included).
G_DRATE.affected_equations_or_variables: The discount rate is taken into consideration when constructing the objective function discounting multiplier (OBJ_DISC), which is applied in each components of the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR, EQ_OBJINV, EQ_OBJFIX, EQ_OBJSALV, EQ_OBJELS).

G_DYEAR.units_ranges_defaults: Year [BOTIME,EOTIME]; default value = M(MIYR_1), i.e. the first milestone year
G_DYEAR.affected_equations_or_variables: The year to which all costs are to be discounted is taken into consideration when constructing the objective function discounting multiplier  (OBJ_DISC), which is applied in each of the components of the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR, EQ_OBJINV, EQ_OBJFIX, EQ_OBJSALV, EQ_OBJELS).

G_OFFTHD.description: Threshold for considering an *_OFF attribute disabling a process/commodity variable in period.
G_OFFTHD.instances_conditions: Setting G_OFFTHD=1 will make the *_OFF attributes effective only for periods fully included in the OFF range specified.
G_OFFTHD.affected_equations_or_variables: Affects availability of VAR_NCAP, VAR_ACT, VAR_FLO, VAR_COMNET/PRD

G_OVERLAP.instances_conditions: Used only when time-stepped solution is activated with the TIMESTEP control variable.

G_TLIFE.description: Default value for the technical lifetime of a process if not provided by the user.

G_YRFR.description: Duration of timeslice s as fraction of a year. Used for shaping the load curve and lining up timeslice duration for inter-regional exchanges.
G_YRFR.units_ranges_defaults: Fraction [0,1]; default value: none; only for the ANNUAL timeslice a value of 1 is predefined
G_YRFR.instances_conditions: Must be provided for each region and timeslice.
G_YRFR.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to various variables (VAR_NCAP+PASTI, VAR_COMX, VAR_IRE, VAR_FLO, VAR_SIN/OUT) in the commodity balance equation (EQ(l)_COMBAL).

GenUCByReg.description: Special: To flag User Constraints that should be generated by Region

IRE_BND.description: Bound on the total import (export) of commodity (c) from (to) region all_r in (out of) region r.
IRE_BND.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: MIG
IRE_BND.instances_conditions: Only applicable for inter-regional exchange processes (IRE). If the bound is specified for a timeslice (s) being above the commodity (c) timeslice resolution, the bound is applied to the sum of the imports/exports according to the timeslice tree. Standard aggregation.
IRE_BND.affected_equations_or_variables: Controls the instances for which the trade bound constraint (EQ(l)_IREBND) is generated, and the RHS.

IRE_CCVT.description: Conversion factor between commodity units in region r1 and region r2. Expresses the amount of commodity c2 in region r2 equivalent to 1 unit of commodity c1 in region r1.
IRE_CCVT.units_ranges_defaults: Scalar (0,?) Default value: 1 if commodity names are the same in both regions I/e: N/A
IRE_CCVT.instances_conditions: Required for mapping commodities involved in inter-regional exchanges between two regions whenever commodities traded are in different units in the regions.
IRE_CCVT.affected_equations_or_variables: The conversion factor is applied to the flow variable (VAR_IRE) in the inter-regional balance constraint (EQ_IRE). Similarly, applied to the flow variable (VAR_IRE) when an inter-regional exchange is bounded in the limit constraint (EQ(l)_IREBND). Similarly, applied to the flow variable (VAR_IRE) when an exchange with an external region is bounded (EQ(l)_XBND).

IRE_FLO.description: Efficiency of exchange process from commodity c1 in region r1 to commodity c2 in the region2 in timeslice s2; the timeslice s2 refers to the r2 region.
IRE_FLO.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit c2/commodity unit c1 [0,?); default value: 1 Default i/e: STD
IRE_FLO.instances_conditions: Only applicable for inter-regional exchange processes (IRE) between two internal regions. Note that for each direction of trade a separate IRE_FLO needs to be specified. Similar to FLO_FUNC for standard processes. Direct inheritance. Weighted aggregation.
IRE_FLO.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the exchange flow variable (VAR_IRE) in the inter-regional trade equation (EQ_IRE).  Applied to the exchange flow variable (VAR_IRE) when a bound on inter-regional trade is to be applied (EQ(l)_IREBND).

IRE_FLOSUM.description: Auxiliary consumption (io = IN, owing to the commodity entering the process) or production/ emission (io = OUT, owing to the commodity leaving the process) of commodity c2 due to the IMPort / EXPort (index ie) of the commodity c1 in region r[8]
IRE_FLOSUM.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit c2/commodity unit c1 [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
IRE_FLOSUM.instances_conditions: Only applicable for inter-regional exchange processes (IRE). Since the efficiency IRE_FLO can only be used for exchange between internal regions, IRE_FLOSUM may be used to define an efficiency for an import/export with an external region by specifying the same commodity for c1 and c2 and the value 1-efficiency as auxiliary consumption. Direct inheritance. Weighted aggregation.
IRE_FLOSUM.affected_equations_or_variables: The multiplier is applied to the flow variable (VAR_IRE) associated with an inter-reginal exchange in the commodity balance constraint (EQ(l)_COMBAL). If a flow share (FLO_SHAR) is provided for an inter-regional exchange process then the multiplier is applied to the flow variable (VAR_IRE) in the share constraint (EQ(l)_IN/OUTSHR). If a cost is provided for the flow (FLO_COST or FLO_DELIV) then the factor is applied to the flow variable (VAR_IRE) in the variable component of the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR).

IRE_PRICE.description: IMPort/EXPort price (index ie) for to/from an internal region of a commodity (c) originating from/heading to an external region all_r.
IRE_PRICE.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit / commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
IRE_PRICE.instances_conditions: Only applicable for inter-regional exchange processes (IRE). Ignored if all_r is an internal region. Direct inheritance. Weighted aggregation.
IRE_PRICE.affected_equations_or_variables: The price of the exchange commodity is applied to the trade flow variable (VAR_IRE) in the variable costs component of the objective function (EQ_OBJVAR).

IRE_TSCVT.description: Matrix for mapping timeslices; the value for (r1,s1,r2,s2) gives the fraction of timeslice s2 in region r2 that falls in timeslice s1 in region r1.
IRE_TSCVT.units_ranges_defaults: Scalar (0,?); default value: 1 if timeslice tree and names are the same in both regions I/e: N/A
IRE_TSCVT.instances_conditions: Used for mapping timeslices in different regions. Required if timeslice definitions are different in the regions.
IRE_TSCVT.affected_equations_or_variables: The conversion factor is applied to the flow variable (VAR_IRE) in the inter-regional balance constraint (EQ_IRE). Similarly, applied to the flow variable (VAR_IRE) when an inter-regional exchange is bounded in the limit constraint (EQ(l)_IREBND). Similarly, applied to the flow variable (VAR_IRE) when an exchange with an external region is bounded (EQ(l)_XBND).

IRE_XBND.description: Bound on the total IMPort (EXPort) (index ie) of commodity c in region all_r with all sources (destinations).
IRE_XBND.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: MIG
IRE_XBND.instances_conditions: Only applicable for inter-regional exchange processes (IRE). Provide whenever a trade flow is to be constrained. Note that the limit is either imposed by summing lower or splitting higher flow variables (VAR_IRE) when specified at other than the actual flow level (as determined by the commodity and process levels (COM_TSL/ PRC_TSL ).
IRE_XBND.affected_equations_or_variables: The trade limit equation EQ(l)_XBND generated either sums lower flow variables (VAR_IRE) or splits (according to the timeslice tree) coarser variables.

IRE_XXBND.description: IRE_XBND with Region declaration in Other_Indexes (external regions possible)

MARKAL-REH.description: Ratio of electricity produced to heat produced

MULTI.description: Multiplier table used for any shaping parameters (*_*M) to adjust the corresponding technical data as function of the year; the table contains different multiplier curves identified by the index j.
MULTI.units_ranges_defaults: Scalar [open]; default value: none I/e: Full dense interpolation and extrapolation
MULTI.instances_conditions: Only provided when the related shaping parameters are to be used.

MakeCorridorPrc.description: Flag to make declare a process as a corridor process in a region

NCAP_AF.description: Availability factor relating a unit of production (process activity) in timeslice s to the current installed capacity.
NCAP_AF.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,1]; default value:  1 Default i/e: STD Remark: In special cases values >1 can also be used (when PRC_CAPACT does not represent the max. technical level of activity per unit of capacity).
NCAP_AF.instances_conditions: NCAP_AF, NCAP_AFA and NCAP_AFS can be applied simultaneously. Direct inheritance. Weighted aggregation. (Important remark: No inheritance/aggregation if any value is specified at process timeslices.)
NCAP_AF.affected_equations_or_variables: The corresponding capacity-activity constraint (EQ(l)_CAPACT) will be generated for any  timeslice s.  If the process timeslice level (PRC_TSL) is below said level, the activity variables will be summed.

NCAP_AFA.description: Annual availability factor relating the annual activity of a process to the installed capacity.
NCAP_AFA.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,1]; default value: none Default i/e: STD  Remark: In special cases values >1 can also be used (when PRC_CAPACT has been chosen not to represent the max. technical level of activity per unit of capacity).
NCAP_AFA.instances_conditions: Provided when ‘ANNUAL’ level process operation is to be controlled. NCAP_AF, NCAP_AFA and NCAP_AFS can be applied simultaneously. NCAP_AFA is always assumed to be non-vintage dependent, even if the process is defined as a vintaged one; for vintage-dependent annual availability NCAP_AFS with s=’ANNUAL’ can be used.
NCAP_AFA.affected_equations_or_variables: The corresponding capacity-activity constraint (EQ(l)_CAPACT) will be generated for the ‘ANNUAL’ timeslice.  If the process timeslice level (PRC_TSL) is below said level, the activity variables will be summed.

NCAP_AFC.description: Commodity-specific availability of capacity for commodity group cg, at given timeslice level.
NCAP_AFC.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_AFC.instances_conditions: If the commodities are in the PCG, constraint is applied to the flows in the PCG as a whole (linear combination of flows). Independent equations are generated for commodities not in the PCG, or when NCAP_AFC(r,’0’,p,’ACT’,tsl)=–1 is also specified.
NCAP_AFC.affected_equations_or_variables: Generates instances of EQ(l)_CAFLAC (thereby disabling EQ(l)_CAPACT generation), or EQL_CAPFLO.

NCAP_AFCS.description: Commodity-specific availability of capacity for commodity group cg, timeslice-specific.
NCAP_AFCS.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_AFCS.instances_conditions: See NCAP_AFC. NCAP_AFCS is similar to NCAP_AFC but is defined on individual timeslices. Overrides NCAP_AFC.

NCAP_AFM.description: Period sensitive multiplier curve (MULTI) to be applied to the availability factor parameters   (NCAP_AF/AFA/AFS) of a process.
NCAP_AFM.units_ranges_defaults: Integer number Default value: 0 (no multiplier applied) Default extrapolation: MIG
NCAP_AFM.instances_conditions: Provided when multiplication of NCAP_AF / NCAP_AFS based upon year is desired. Note: Multiplier index 1 is reserved for constant 1.

NCAP_AFS.description: Availability factor relating the activity of a process in a timeslice s being at or above the process timeslice level (prc_tsl) to the installed capacity. If for example the process timeslice level is ‘DAYNITE’ and NCAP_AFS is specified for timeslices on the ‘SEASONAL’ level, the sum of the ‘DAYNITE’ activities within a season are restricted, but not the ‘DAYNITE’ activities directly.
NCAP_AFS.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,1]; default value: none Default i/e: STD  Remark: In special cases values >1 can also be used (in cases where PRC_CAPACT has been chosen not to represent the maximum technical level of activity per unit of capacity).
NCAP_AFS.instances_conditions: NCAP_AF, NCAP_AFA and NCAP_AFS can be applied simultaneously. NCAP_AFS being specified for timeslices s being below the process timeslice level are ignored. No inheritance. No aggregation.  Can be used also on the process timeslices, and will then override the levelized NCAP_AF availability factors.
NCAP_AFS.affected_equations_or_variables: The corresponding capacity-activity constraint (EQ(l)_CAPACT) will be generated for a timeslice s being at or above the process timeslice level (prc_tsl). If the process timeslice level is below said level, the activity variables will be summed.

NCAP_AFX.description: Age-based shaping curve (SHAPE) to be applied to the availability factor parameters   (NCAP_AF/AFA/AFS) of a process.
NCAP_AFX.units_ranges_defaults: Integer number Default value: 0 (no shape curve applied) Default extrapolation: MIG
NCAP_AFX.instances_conditions: Provided when shaping based upon age is desired. NCAP_AFX is applied to NCAP_AF and NCAP_AFS, but not the annual availability NCAP_AFA. For non-vintaged process, the SHAPE parameter is only applied to NCAP_AF, i.e. availabilities at process timeslices will be vintaged. Note: Shape index 1 is reserved for constant 1.

NCAP_BND.description: Bound on the permitted level on investment in new capacity
NCAP_BND.units_ranges_defaults: Capacity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: MIG
NCAP_BND.instances_conditions: Provided for each process to have its overall installed capacity (VAR_NCAP) limited in a period. Since inter-/extrapolation default is MIG, a bound must be specified for each period desired, if no explicit inter-/extrapolation option is given, e.g. NCAP_BND(R,’0’,P) =2.
NCAP_BND.affected_equations_or_variables: Imposes an indirect limit on the capacity transfer equation (EQ_CPT) by means of a direct bound on the new investments capacity variable (VAR_NCAP).

NCAP_BPME.description: Back pressure mode efficiency (or total efficiency in full CHP mode).
NCAP_BPME.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_BPME.instances_conditions: The parameter is only taken into account when the process is of type CHP, and NCAP_CDME has been also defined.
NCAP_BPME.affected_equations_or_variables: Process transformation equation, either  EQE_ACTEFF or EQ_PTRANS

NCAP_CDME.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_CDME.instances_conditions: The parameter can only be used for standard process­es having electricity output in the PCG. The efficiency is applied between the default shadow group and the electricity. If the process is also defined as a CHP, heat efficiency is also included.
NCAP_CDME.affected_equations_or_variables: Process transformation equation, either  EQE_ACTEFF or EQ_PTRANS

NCAP_CEH.description: Coefficient of electricity to heat along the iso-fuel line in a pass-out CHP technology.
NCAP_CEH.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [–1,?]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_CEH.instances_conditions: The parameter is only taken into account when the process is defined to be of type CHP. According to the CEH value, the process activity will be defined as:  CEH ? 0: Max. electricity output according to CHPR 0 < CEH ?1: Condensing mode electricity output  CEH ? 1: Total energy output in full CHP mode.
NCAP_CEH.affected_equations_or_variables: Process transformation equation, either  EQE_ACTEFF or EQ_PTRANS

NCAP_CHPR.description: Heat-to-power ratio of a CHP technology (fixed / minimum / maximum ratio). If no ratio equations should be generated, one can define any I/E value with lim='N'.
NCAP_CHPR.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction  [0,?);   default value: 1 (only when process type is CHP, for lim='UP') Default i/e: STD
NCAP_CHPR.instances_conditions: The parameter is only taken into account when the process is defined to be of type CHP. The defaults can be disabled by defining any i/e value with lim='N', which will eliminate the output share equations.
NCAP_CHPR.affected_equations_or_variables: Activates the generation of output share equations, implemented with EQ(l)_OUTSHR

NCAP_CLAG.description: Lagtime of a commodity after new capacity is installed.
NCAP_CLAG.units_ranges_defaults: Years [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_CLAG.instances_conditions: Provided when there is a delay in commodity output after commissioning new capacity. So, if the process is available in the year K, the commodity is produced during the years [K+CLAG, K+NCAP_TLIFE–1].
NCAP_CLAG.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the investment variable (VAR_NCAP) in the commodity balance (EQ(l)_COMBAL) of the investment period or previous periods.

NCAP_CLED.description: Lead time requirement for a commodity during construction (NCAP_ICOM), prior to the initial availability of the capacity.
NCAP_CLED.units_ranges_defaults: Years [open]; default value: = NCAP_ILED Default i/e: STD
NCAP_CLED.instances_conditions: Provided when a commodity must be available prior to availability of a process. So, if the process is available in the year B(v) +NCAP_ILED–1, the commodity is produced during the time span [B(v)+ILED–CLED, B(v) +NCAP_ILED–1]. Usually used when modelling the need for fabrication of reactor fuel the period before a reactor goes online.
NCAP_CLED.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the investment variable (VAR_NCAP) in the commodity balance (EQ(l)_COMBAL) of the investment period or previous periods.

NCAP_COM.description: Emission (or land-use) of commodity c associated with the capacity of a process for each year said capacity exists.
NCAP_COM.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit per capacity unit [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_COM.instances_conditions: Provided when the consumption or production of a commodity is tied to the level of the installed capacity.
NCAP_COM.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the capacity variable (VAR_CAP) in the commodity balance (EQ_COMBAL).

NCAP_COST.description: Investment costs of new installed capacity according to the installation year.
NCAP_COST.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per capacity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_COST.instances_conditions: Provided whenever there is a cost associated with putting new capacity in place.
NCAP_COST.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the investment variable (VAR_NCAP) when entering the objective function (EQ_OBJNV). May appear in user constraints (EQ_UC*) if specified in UC_NAME.

NCAP_CPX.description: Defines a shape index for shaping the capacity transfer coefficients by the age of each process vintage. As a result, the capacity will have a survival rate as a function of age.
NCAP_CPX.units_ranges_defaults: Integer number  Default value: 0  (no shape curve applied)  Default extrapolation:  MIG
NCAP_CPX.instances_conditions: Provided when shaping based upon age is desired.  The SHAPE index given by NCAP_CPX is applied to the internal capacity transfer parameter (COEF_CPT).  Note: Shape index 1 is reserved for constant 1.
NCAP_CPX.affected_equations_or_variables: Impacts all calculations  that are dependent  upon the availability of  capacity (VAR_NCAP), most directly the capacity transfer (EQ_CPT), and capacity availability equations  (EQ(l)_CAPACT).

NCAP_DCOST.description: Cost of dismantling a facility after the end of its lifetime.
NCAP_DCOST.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per capacity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_DCOST.instances_conditions: Provided when there are decommissioning costs associated with a process. Decommissioning of a process and the payment of decommissioning costs may be delayed by a lag time (NCAP_DLAG).
NCAP_DCOST.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the current capacity subject to decommissioning (VAR_NCAP+NCAP_PASTI) when entering the objective function (EQ_OBJNV).

NCAP_DELIF.description: Economic lifetime of the decommissioning activity.
NCAP_DELIF.units_ranges_defaults: Years (0,?); default value: NCAP_DLIFE Default i/e: STD
NCAP_DELIF.instances_conditions: Provided when the timeframe for paying for decommission is different from that of the actual decommissioning.
NCAP_DELIF.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the investment variable (VAR_NCAP) when entering the salvage portion of the objective function (EQ_OBJSALV).

NCAP_DISC.description: Size of capacity units that can be added.
NCAP_DISC.units_ranges_defaults: Capacity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: MIG
NCAP_DISC.instances_conditions: Used for lumpy investments. Requires MIP. Since inter-/extrapolation default is MIG, a value must be specified for each period desired, if no explicit inter-/extrapolation option is given.
NCAP_DISC.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the lumpy investment integer variable (VAR_DNCAP) in the discrete investment equation (EQ_DSCNCAP) to set the corresponding standard investment variable level (VAR_NCAP).

NCAP_DLAG.description: Number of years delay before decommissioning can begin after the lifetime of a technology has ended.
NCAP_DLAG.units_ranges_defaults: Years [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_DLAG.instances_conditions: Provided when there is a lag in the decommissioning of a process (e.g., to allow the nuclear core to reduce its radiation).
NCAP_DLAG.affected_equations_or_variables: Delay applied to a decommissioning flow (VAR_FLO) in the balance equation (EQ(l)_COMBAL) as production. Delay applied to the current capacity subject to decommissioning (VAR_NCAP+NCAP_PASTI) when entering the objective function components (EQ_OBJINV, EQ_OBJFIX,  EQ_OBJSALV).

NCAP_DLAGC.description: Cost occurring during the lag time after the technical lifetime of a process has ended and before its decommissioning starts.
NCAP_DLAGC.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per capacity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_DLAGC.instances_conditions: Provided when there is a cost during any lag in the decommissioning (e.g., security).
NCAP_DLAGC.affected_equations_or_variables: Cost during delay applied to the current capacity subject to decommissioning (VAR_NCAP+NCAP_PASTI) when entering the objective function components (EQ_OBJFIX,  EQ_OBJSALV).

NCAP_DLIFE.description: Technical time for dismantling a facility after the end its technical lifetime, plus any lag time (NCAP_DLAG).
NCAP_DLIFE.units_ranges_defaults: Years (0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_DLIFE.instances_conditions: Provided when a process has a decommissioning phase.
NCAP_DLIFE.affected_equations_or_variables: Decommissioning time impacting (VAR_NCAP+NCAP_PASTI) when entering the objective function components (EQ_OBJINV, EQ_OBJSALV).

NCAP_DRATE.units_ranges_defaults: Percent (0,?); default value: G_DRATE Default i/e: STD
NCAP_DRATE.instances_conditions: Provided if the cost of borrowing for a process is different from the standard discount rate.
NCAP_DRATE.affected_equations_or_variables: Discount rate applied to investments (VAR_NCAP+NCAP_PASTI) when entering the objective function components (EQ_OBJINV, EQ_OBJSALV).

NCAP_EFFX.description: Change in efficiency from the year of initial installation based upon the age of the plant.The value entered corresponds to the index into the SHAPE  table.

NCAP_ELIFE.units_ranges_defaults: years (0,?); default value: NCAP_TLIFE Default i/e: STD
NCAP_ELIFE.instances_conditions: Provided only when the economic lifetime differs from the technical lifetime (NCAP_TLIFE).
NCAP_ELIFE.affected_equations_or_variables: Economic lifetime of a process when costing investment (VAR_NCAP+NCAP_PASTI) or capacity in the objective function components (EQ_OBJINV, EQ_OBJSALV, EQ_OBJFIX).

NCAP_FDR.description: Defines an annual rate of additional depreciation in the salvage value.
NCAP_FDR.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction (0,?); default value:none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_FDR.instances_conditions: Provided when the effect of functional depreciation is considered significant to justify accelerated decrease in salvage value.
NCAP_FDR.affected_equations_or_variables: Affects the salvage value coefficients in EQ_OBJSALV

NCAP_FOM.description: Fixed operating and maintenance cost per unit of capacity according to the installation year.
NCAP_FOM.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per capacity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_FOM.instances_conditions: Provided when there is a fixed cost associated with the installed capacity.
NCAP_FOM.affected_equations_or_variables: Fixed operating and maintenance costs associated with total installed capacity (VAR_NCAP+NCAP_PASTI) when entering the objective function components (EQ_OBJFIX).

NCAP_FOMM.description: Period sensitive multiplier curve (MULTI) applied to the fixed operating and maintenance costs (NCAP_FOM).
NCAP_FOMM.units_ranges_defaults: Integer number Default value: 0 (no multiplier curve applied) Default i/e: MIG
NCAP_FOMM.instances_conditions: Provided when shaping based upon the period is desired. Note: Multiplier index 1 is reserved for constant 1.

NCAP_FOMX.description: Age-based shaping curve (SHAPE) to be applied to the fixed operating and maintenance cost.
NCAP_FOMX.units_ranges_defaults: Integer number Default value: 0 (no shape curve applied) Default i/e: MIG
NCAP_FOMX.instances_conditions: Provided when shaping based upon age is desired. Note: Shape index 1 is reserved for constant 1.

NCAP_FSUB.description: Subsidy per unit of installed capacity.
NCAP_FSUB.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit per capacity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_FSUB.instances_conditions: Provided when there is a subsidy for associated with the level of installed capacity.
NCAP_FSUB.affected_equations_or_variables: Fixed subsidy associated with total installed capacity (VAR_NCAP+NCAP_PASTI) when entering the objective function component (EQ_OBJFIX) with a minus sign.

NCAP_FSUBM.description: Period sensitive multiplier curve (MULTI) applied to the subsidy (NCAP_FSUB).
NCAP_FSUBM.units_ranges_defaults: Integer number Default value: 0 (no multiplier curve applied) Default i/e: MIG
NCAP_FSUBM.instances_conditions: Provided when shaping based upon the period is desired. Note: Multiplier index 1 is reserved for constant 1.

NCAP_FSUBX.description: Age-based shaping curve (SHAPE) to be applied to the fixed subsidy (NCAP_FSUB).
NCAP_FSUBX.units_ranges_defaults: Integer number Default value: 0 (no shape curve applied) Default i/e: MIG
NCAP_FSUBX.instances_conditions: Provided when shaping based upon age is desired. Note: Shape index 1 is reserved for constant 1.

NCAP_FTAX.description: Tax per unit of installed capacity.
NCAP_FTAX.units_ranges_defaults: monetary unit per capacity unit [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_FTAX.instances_conditions: Provided when there is a fixed tax based upon the level of the installed capacity.
NCAP_FTAX.affected_equations_or_variables: Fixed subsidy associated with total installed capacity (VAR_NCAP+NCAP_PASTI) when entering the objective function components (EQ_OBJFIX).

NCAP_FTAXM.description: Period sensitive multiplier curve (MULTI) applied to the tax (NCAP_FTAX).
NCAP_FTAXM.units_ranges_defaults: Integer number Default value: 0 (no multiplier curve applied) Default i/e: MIG
NCAP_FTAXM.instances_conditions: Provided when shaping based upon the period is desired. Note: Multiplier index 1 is reserved for constant 1.

NCAP_FTAXX.description: Age-based shaping curve (SHAPE) to be applied to the fixed tax (NCAP_FTAX).
NCAP_FTAXX.units_ranges_defaults: Integer number Default value: 0 (no shape curve applied) Default i/e: MIG
NCAP_FTAXX.instances_conditions: Provided when shaping based upon age is desired. Note: Shape index 1 is reserved for constant 1.

NCAP_ICOM.description: Amount of commodity (c) required for the construction of new capacity.
NCAP_ICOM.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit per capacity unit [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_ICOM.instances_conditions: Provided when a commodity is needed in the period in which the new capacity is to be available, or before NCAP_CLED. If NCAP_CLED is provided, the commodity is required during the years [B(v)+NCAP_CLED,B(v)+NCAP_ILED-NCAP_CLED]. If this time spans more than one period, the commodity flow is split up proportion­ally between the periods. For the commodity balance the commodity requirement in a period is converted to an average annual commodity flow for the entire period, although the construction may take place only for a few years of the period. Negative value describes production (e.g. emissions) at the time of a new investment.
NCAP_ICOM.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the investment variable (VAR_NCAP) in the appropriate commodity constraints (EQ(l)_COMBAL) as part of consumption.

NCAP_ILED.description: Lead time between investment decision and actual availability of new capacity (= construction time).
NCAP_ILED.units_ranges_defaults: Years [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_ILED.instances_conditions: Provided when there is a delay between when the investment decision occurs and when the capacity (new capacity or past investment) is initially available. If NCAP_ILED>0, the investment decision is assumed to occur at B(v) and the capacity becomes available at B(v)+NCAP-ILED. If NCAP_ILED<0, the investment decision is assumed to occur at B(v)-NCAP_ILED and the capacity becomes available at B(v). Causes an IDC overhead in the investment costs accounting.
NCAP_ILED.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the investment variable (VAR_NCAP) balance constraints (EQ(l)_COMBAL) as part of consumption, if there is an associated flow (NCAP_ICOM). Used as to distinguish between small and large investments (VAR_NCAP) and thus influences the way the investment and fixed costs are treated in the objective function (EQ_OBJINV, EQ_OBJFIX, EQ_OBJSALV).

NCAP_ISPCT.description: Subsidy as % of new investment cost

NCAP_ISUB.description: Subsidy per unit of new installed capacity.
NCAP_ISUB.units_ranges_defaults: monetary unit per capacity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_ISUB.instances_conditions: Provided when there is a subsidy for new investments in a period.
NCAP_ISUB.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the investment variable (VAR_NCAP) when entering the objective function (EQ_OBJNV) with a minus sign. May appear in user constraints (EQ_UC*) if specified in UC_NAME.

NCAP_ITAX.description: Tax per unit of new installed capacity
NCAP_ITAX.units_ranges_defaults: monetary unit per capacity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_ITAX.instances_conditions: Provided when there is a tax associated with new investments in a period.
NCAP_ITAX.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the investment variable (VAR_NCAP) when entering the objective function (EQ_OBJNV). May appear in user constraints (EQ_UC*) if specified in UC_NAME.

NCAP_MSPRF.description: lim = 'N' : Defines the preference weights αk in the logit market share formulation for the market com, and technology prc. Default value = 1; lim = 'LO' : Defines the intangible costs as a fraction of the investment costs for the market com, and technology prc. Default value = 0; lim = 'UP' : Defines the dummy penalty costs in proportion to the capacity-related costs for the market com, and technology prc. Default value = 2.

NCAP_OCOM.description: Amount of commodity c per unit of capacity released during the dismantling of a process.
NCAP_OCOM.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit per capacity unit [open]; default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_OCOM.instances_conditions: Provided when there is a commodity release associated with the decommissioning. The year index of the parameter corresponds to the vintage year. If the decommissioning time (NCAP_DLIFE) falls in more than one period, is split up proportionally among the periods. For the commodity balance the commodity release in a period is converted to an average annual commodity flow for the entire period, although the dismantling may take place only for a few years of the period.
NCAP_OCOM.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the investment variable (VAR_NCAP) in the appropriate commodity constraints (EQ(l)_COMBAL) as part of production in the appropriate period.

NCAP_OLIFE.description: Maximum operating lifetime of a process, in terms of full-load years.
NCAP_OLIFE.units_ranges_defaults: Years (0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_OLIFE.instances_conditions: Requires that early retirements are enabled and the process is vintaged.

NCAP_PASTI.description: Investment in new capacity made before the beginning of the model horizon (in the year specified by pastyear).
NCAP_PASTI.units_ranges_defaults: capacity unit [0,?); default value: none No i/e
NCAP_PASTI.instances_conditions: Past investment can also be specified for milestone years, e.g. if the milestone year is a historic year, so that capacity additions are known or if planned future investments are already known.

NCAP_PASTY.description: Number of years to go back to calculate a linear build-up of past investments
NCAP_PASTY.units_ranges_defaults: Years [1,999]; default value: none No i/e
NCAP_PASTY.instances_conditions: Provided to spread a single past investment (NCAP_PASTI) back over several years (e.g., cars in the period before the 1st milestoneyr were bought over the previous 15 years). If overlaps with other past investments, the capacity values are added.

NCAP_PKCNT.description: Fraction of capacity that can contribute to peaking equations.
NCAP_PKCNT.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [0,1]; default value: 1 Default i/e: STD
NCAP_PKCNT.instances_conditions: If the indicator PRC_PKAF is specified, the NCAP_PKCNT is set equal to the availabilities NCAP_AF. Direct inheritance. Weighted aggregation.
NCAP_PKCNT.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to investments in capacity (VAR_NCAP, NCAP_PASTI) in the peaking constraint (EQ_PEAK).

NCAP_SEMI.description: Semi-continuous new capacity, lower bound. (See Section 5.9)
NCAP_SEMI.units_ranges_defaults: Capacity unit (0,?); default value: none Default i/e: MIG
NCAP_SEMI.instances_conditions: Upper bound for the capacity must be defined by NCAP_BND; if not defined, assumed to be equal to the lower bound. Requires MIP.
NCAP_SEMI.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the semi-continuous investment variable VAR_SNCAP in the discrete investment equation EQ_DSCNCAP

NCAP_START.affected_equations_or_variables: Affects the availability of investment variable (VAR_NCAP)

NCAP_TLIFE.units_ranges_defaults: Years (0,?); default value: G_TLIFE Default i/e: STD
NCAP_TLIFE.instances_conditions: Expected for all technologies that have investment costs. Values below 0.5 cannot be well accounted in the objective function, and should thus be avoided (they are automatically resetted to 1).
NCAP_TLIFE.affected_equations_or_variables: Impacts all calculations that are dependent upon the availability of investments (VAR_NCAP) including capacity transfer (EQ_CPT), commodity flow (EQ(l)_COMBAL), costs (EQ_OBJINV, EQ_OBJFIX, EQ_OBJVAR, EQ_OBJSALV).

NCAP_VALU.description: Value of a commodity released at decommissioning (NCAP_OCOM).
NCAP_VALU.units_ranges_defaults: Monetary unit / commodity unit [0,?); default value: none Default i/e: STD
NCAP_VALU.instances_conditions: Provided when a released commodity has a value.
NCAP_VALU.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the investment related (VAR_NCAP, NCAP_PASTI) release flow at decommissioning in the objective function (EQ_OBJSALV).

OFFEPS.description: Scenario-level flag to declare $OFFEPS at the top of the DD file; $ONEPS appears by default

PRAT.description: The “progress ratio” for a technology that is modeledas one for which endogenous technology learning (ETL)applies

PRC_ACTFLO.description: 1) Conversion factor from units of activity to units of those flow variables that define the activity (primary commodity group), or, 2) Conversion multiplier representing the amount of flow(s) in the cg per 1 unit of activity.
PRC_ACTFLO.units_ranges_defaults: Commodity unit / activity unit (0,?); default value: 1 Default i/e: STD
PRC_ACTFLO.instances_conditions: Only (rarely) provided when either the activity and flow variables of a process are in different units, or if there is a conversion efficiency between the activity and the flow(s) in the PCG. The group (cg) can be the whole PCG or any individual commodity in the PCG, or 'ACT' (=PCG).
PRC_ACTFLO.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied to the primary commodity (prc_pcg) flow variables (VAR_FLO, VAR_IRE) to relate overall activity (VAR_ACT in EQ_ACTFLO). When the Reduction algorithm activated it is applied to the activity variable (VAR_ACT) in those cases where the flow variable (VAR_FLO) can be replaced by the activity variable (e.g. the activity is defined by one commodity flow).

PRC_AOFF.description: Set of quadruples {r,p,y1,y2} such that process p cannot operate (activity is zero) between the years y1 and y2 in region r [this is a switch; >0 => YES]

PRC_CAPACT.description: Conversion factor from capacity unit to activity unit assuming that the capacity is used for one year.
PRC_CAPACT.units_ranges_defaults: Activity unit / capacity unit (0,?); default value: 1 Default i/e: none
PRC_CAPACT.affected_equations_or_variables: Applied along with the availability factor (NCAP_AF) to the investment (VAR_NCAP + NCAP_PASTI) in the utilization equations (EQ(l)_CAPACT, EQ(l)_CAFLAC). Applied to the investment (VAR_NCAP + NCAP_PASTI) in the peak constraint (EQ_PEAK). Applied to the investment (VAR_NCAP + NCAP_PASTI) in the capacity utilization constraint for CHP plants (ECT_AFCHP) and peak constraint in the IER extension (see Part III).

PRC_FOFF.description: Set of sextuples specifying that the flow of commodity c at process p and timeslice s is not available between the years y1 and y2 in region r [this is a switch; >0 => YES]

PRC_MARK.description: Process group-wise market share, which defines a constraint for the combined market share of multiple processes in the total commodity production.
PRC_MARK.units_ranges_defaults: Decimal fraction [open]; default value: none Default i/e: 11
PRC_MARK.instances_conditions: Combined limit on commodity production is derived as the sum of the process-specific productions multiplied by the inverse values of PRC_MARK. The constraint is applied to the annual production of commodity. Item can be a any desired label identifying the group.
